hierarchy.title: भारतीय įरज़वर् बैंक RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
hierarchy.text:
भारतीय įरज़वर् बैंक RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
RBI/DOR/2025-26/318
DOR.ORG.REC.No.237/21-04-158/2025-26


November 28, 2025
Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Managing Risks in
Outsourcing) Directions, 2025

children.title: Table of Contents
children.text: Table of Contents

children.title: <image>
G. Redressal of Grievances related to Outsourced Services ........................................... 17
children.text: G. Redressal of Grievances related to Outsourced Services ........................................... 17

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
In exercise of the powers conferred by Section 35A read with Section 56 of
the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, as amended vide Banking Regulation (Amendment)
Act 2020 (39 of 2020), and all other provisions / laws enabling the Reserve Bank of
India ('RBI') in this regard, RBI being satisfied that it is necessary and expedient in the
public interest so to do, hereby, issues the Directions hereinafter specified.

children.title: A. Short Title and Commencement
children.text: A. Short Title and Commencement

children.title: 1. These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative
children.text:
1. These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative
Banks - Managing Risks in Outsourcing) Directions, 2025.

children.title: 2. These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
children.text: 2. These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.

children.title: B. Applicability and Scope
children.text: B. Applicability and Scope

children.title: 3. These Directions shall be applicable to all Rural Co-operative Banks, hereinafter
children.text:
3. These Directions shall be applicable to all Rural Co-operative Banks, hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'RCBs' and individually as an 'RCB'.
For the purpose of these Directions, 'Rural Co-operative Banks' mean State Co-
operative Banks and Central Co-operative Banks, as defined in the National Bank
for Agriculture and Rural Development Act, 1981.

children.title: 4. These Directions apply to outsourcing arrangements entered in by an RCB with a
children.text:
4. These Directions apply to outsourcing arrangements entered in by an RCB with a
service provider, located in India or elsewhere, for outsourcing of financial services
like applications processing (loan origination, credit card), document processing,
marketing and research, supervision of loans, data processing, and back office
related activities.

children.title: 5. These Directions shall not apply to outsourcing of activities unrelated to banking
children.text:
5. These Directions shall not apply to outsourcing of activities unrelated to banking
services like usage of courier, catering of staff, housekeeping and janitorial services,
security of the premises, movement and archiving of records.

children.title: C. Definitions
children.text: C. Definitions

children.title: 6. In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires,
children.text: 6. In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires,

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(1) 'Outsourcing' means use of a third party by the RCB to perform activities on a
continuing basis that would normally be undertaken by the RCB itself, now or in
the future. 'Continuing basis' shall include agreements for a limited period.
(2) 'Material Outsourcing' means arrangements, which if disrupted, have the
potential to significantly impact the business operations, reputation, or profitability
of an RCB. 'Materiality' of outsourcing shall be based on the:

children.title: (i) level of importance to the RCB of the activity being outsourced as well as the
children.text:
(i) level of importance to the RCB of the activity being outsourced as well as the
significance of the risk posed by the same;

children.title: (ii) potential impact of the outsourcing on the RCB on various parameters such
children.text:
(ii) potential impact of the outsourcing on the RCB on various parameters such
as earnings, solvency, liquidity, funding capital, and risk profile;

children.title: (iii) likely impact on the RCB's reputation and brand value, and ability to achieve
(iv) cost of the outsourcing as a proportion of total operating costs of the RCB;
children.text: (iv) cost of the outsourcing as a proportion of total operating costs of the RCB;

children.title: (v) aggregate exposure to that particular service provider, in cases where the
children.text:
(v) aggregate exposure to that particular service provider, in cases where the
RCB outsources various functions to the same service provider; and

children.title: (vi) significance of activities outsourced by RCB in the context of customer
children.text:
(vi) significance of activities outsourced by RCB in the context of customer
service and protection.

children.title: 7. All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as have
children.text:
7. All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as have
been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the Reserve Bank
of India Act, 1934 or the Companies Act, 2013 and Rules made thereunder, or any
statutory modification or re-enactment thereto, or Glossary of Terms published by
RBI or as used in commercial parlance, as the case may be.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: Chapter II - Role of the Board
hierarchy.text: Chapter II - Role of the Board

children.title: 8. The outsourcing of any activity by an RCB does not diminish its obligations, and
children.text:
8. The outsourcing of any activity by an RCB does not diminish its obligations, and
those of its Board and Managing Director (MD) / Chief Executive Officer (CEO)
along with the Senior Management, who have the ultimate responsibility for the
outsourced activity.

children.title: A. Board-Approved Policy
children.text: A. Board-Approved Policy

children.title: 9. An RCB intending to outsource any of its financial services shall put in place a
children.text:
9. An RCB intending to outsource any of its financial services shall put in place a
comprehensive Board-approved outsourcing policy, the coverage of which is
indicated in paragraph 16.

children.title: B. Key responsibilities
children.text: B. Key responsibilities

children.title: 10. The Board shall be responsible for putting in place a framework to evaluate the risks
children.text:
10. The Board shall be responsible for putting in place a framework to evaluate the risks
and materiality of all existing and prospective outsourcing arrangements, laying
down appropriate approval authorities depending on risks and materiality, and
undertaking regular review. It shall be responsible, inter alia, for:

children.title: (i) approving a framework to evaluate the risks and materiality of all existing and
(ii) laying down appropriate approval authorities for outsourcing depending on risks
children.text:
(ii) laying down appropriate approval authorities for outsourcing depending on risks
and materiality;

children.title: (iii) undertaking regular review of the framework for its efficacy and updating the
(iv) deciding on business activities of a material nature to be outsourced and
children.text:
(iv) deciding on business activities of a material nature to be outsourced and
approving such arrangements;

children.title: (v) assessing management competencies to develop sound and responsive
(vi) setting up suitable administrative framework of management;
children.text: (vi) setting up suitable administrative framework of management;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (vii) reviewing records of all material outsourcing on half-yearly basis;
children.text: (vii) reviewing records of all material outsourcing on half-yearly basis;

children.title: (viii) ensuring that a robust system of internal audit of all outsourced financial
children.text:
(viii) ensuring that a robust system of internal audit of all outsourced financial
activities is put in place and monitoring the same; and

children.title: (ix) ensuring submission of an Annual Compliance Certificate giving the particulars
children.text:
(ix) ensuring submission of an Annual Compliance Certificate giving the particulars
of contracts for outsourcing of financial services, the prescribed periodicity of
audit by internal / external auditor, major findings of the audit, and action taken,
to NABARD.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: Chapter III - Outsourcing of Financial Services
hierarchy.text: Chapter III - Outsourcing of Financial Services

children.title: A. Activities that shall not be outsourced
children.text: A. Activities that shall not be outsourced

children.title: 11. An RCB which chooses to outsource financial services shall however not outsource
children.text:
11. An RCB which chooses to outsource financial services shall however not outsource
core management functions including policy formulation, Internal Audit and
compliance, compliance with KYC norms, credit sanction, and management of
investment portfolio.
Provided that with respect to Internal Audit, where required, experts, including
former employees, could be hired on a contractual basis subject to:

children.title: (i) the Audit Committee of Board (ACB) / Board being assured that such expertise
children.text:
(i) the Audit Committee of Board (ACB) / Board being assured that such expertise
does not exist within the audit function of the RCB;

children.title: (ii) any conflict of interest in such matters being recognised and effectively
children.text:
(ii) any conflict of interest in such matters being recognised and effectively
addressed; and

children.title: (iii) ownership of audit reports in all cases resting with regular functionaries of the
children.text:
(iii) ownership of audit reports in all cases resting with regular functionaries of the
internal audit function.

children.title: B. Authorisation, Accountability, and Oversight
children.text: B. Authorisation, Accountability, and Oversight

children.title: 12. An RCB, which desires to outsource financial services, shall not require prior
children.text:
12. An RCB, which desires to outsource financial services, shall not require prior
approval from RBI / NABARD. However, such arrangements shall be subject to on-
site / off-site monitoring and inspection / scrutiny, by RBI / NABARD.

children.title: 13. As stated in paragraph 8 of these Directions, the outsourcing of any activity by an
children.text:
13. As stated in paragraph 8 of these Directions, the outsourcing of any activity by an
RCB shall not diminish its obligations including to its customers and RBI / NABARD,
and those of its Board and MD / CEO along with the Senior Management, who have
the ultimate responsibility for the outsourced activity. An RCB shall, therefore, be
responsible for the actions of its service provider including Business Correspondent
(BC) and its retail outlets / sub-agents and the confidentiality of information
pertaining to the customers that is available with the service provider. An RCB shall
retain ultimate control of the outsourced activity.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 14. An RCB shall ensure that:
children.text: 14. An RCB shall ensure that:

children.title: (i) all relevant laws, regulations, rules, guidelines, and conditions of approval,
(ii) outsourcing, whether the service provider is located in India or outside, does not
children.text:
(ii) outsourcing, whether the service provider is located in India or outside, does not
impede RBI / NABARD in carrying out its regulatory / supervisory functions and
objectives and diminish the ability of an RCB to fulfil its obligations to the
regulator / supervisor;

children.title: (iii) outsourcing, whether the service provider is located in India or outside, does not
(iv) outsourcing would not result in the compromise or weakening of an RCB's
children.text:
(iv) outsourcing would not result in the compromise or weakening of an RCB's
internal control, business conduct, or reputation;

children.title: (v) the service provider employs the same high standard of care in performing the
children.text:
(v) the service provider employs the same high standard of care in performing the
services as would be employed by the RCB, if the activities were conducted
within the RCB and not outsourced; and

children.title: (vi) the service provider shall not be owned or controlled by any director or officer /
children.text:
(vi) the service provider shall not be owned or controlled by any director or officer /
employee of the RCB or their relatives having the same meaning as assigned
under Companies Act, 2013 and the Rules framed thereunder, as amended
from time to time.

children.title: 15. An RCB shall be responsible for filing Currency Transactions Reports (CTRs) and
children.text:
15. An RCB shall be responsible for filing Currency Transactions Reports (CTRs) and
Suspicious Transactions Reports (STRs) to FIU or any other competent authority in
respect of its customer related activities carried out by the service provider.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: C. Governance Framework
children.text:
C. Governance Framework
C.1 Outsourcing Policy

children.title: 16. An RCB intending to outsource any of its financial services shall put in place a
children.text:
16. An RCB intending to outsource any of its financial services shall put in place a
comprehensive outsourcing policy, approved by its Board, which shall incorporate,
inter alia, the following:

children.title: (i) criteria for selection of such activities and the service providers;
children.text: (i) criteria for selection of such activities and the service providers;

children.title: (ii) parameters for defining 'material outsourcing' based on the broad criteria
children.text:
(ii) parameters for defining 'material outsourcing' based on the broad criteria
indicated in paragraph 6(2) of these Directions;

children.title: (iii) delegation of authority depending on risks and materiality; and
children.text: (iii) delegation of authority depending on risks and materiality; and

children.title: (iv) systems to monitor and review these activities.
children.text:
(iv) systems to monitor and review these activities.
C.2 Role of Senior Management

children.title: 17. The MD / CEO and Senior Management of an RCB shall, inter alia, be responsible
children.text:
17. The MD / CEO and Senior Management of an RCB shall, inter alia, be responsible
for:

children.title: (i) evaluating the risks and materiality of all existing and prospective outsourcing,
children.text:
(i) evaluating the risks and materiality of all existing and prospective outsourcing,
based on the framework approved by the Board;

children.title: (ii) developing and implementing sound and prudent outsourcing policy and
(iii) reviewing periodically the effectiveness of policies and procedures;
children.text: (iii) reviewing periodically the effectiveness of policies and procedures;

children.title: (iv) communicating information pertaining to material outsourcing risks to the Board
children.text:
(iv) communicating information pertaining to material outsourcing risks to the Board
in a timely manner;

children.title: (v) ensuring that contingency plans, based on realistic and probable disruptive
children.text:
(v) ensuring that contingency plans, based on realistic and probable disruptive
scenarios, are in place and tested;

children.title: (vi) ensuring that there is independent review and audit for compliance with set
children.text:
(vi) ensuring that there is independent review and audit for compliance with set
policies; and

children.title: (vii) undertaking periodic review of outsourcing arrangements to identify new
children.text:
(vii) undertaking periodic review of outsourcing arrangements to identify new
material outsourcing risks.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: D. Risk Management
children.text:
D. Risk Management
D.1 Evaluation of the Risks

children.title: 18. An RCB shall evaluate and guard against the following key risks when entering into
children.text:
18. An RCB shall evaluate and guard against the following key risks when entering into
outsourcing arrangements:

children.title: (i) Strategic Risk - such as where the service provider conducts business on its
children.text:
(i) Strategic Risk - such as where the service provider conducts business on its
own behalf, inconsistent with the overall strategic goals of the RCB.

children.title: (ii) Reputation Risk - such as where the service provider delivers poor service or
children.text:
(ii) Reputation Risk - such as where the service provider delivers poor service or
its customer interactions are inconsistent with the overall standards of the RCB,
or it fails to preserve and protect confidential customer information.

children.title: (iii) Compliance Risk - such as where, owing to outsourcing, the privacy,
children.text:
(iii) Compliance Risk - such as where, owing to outsourcing, the privacy,
consumer, and prudential laws are not adequately complied with.

children.title: (iv) Operational Risk - which may arise due to technology failure, fraud, error, or
children.text:
(iv) Operational Risk - which may arise due to technology failure, fraud, error, or
inadequate financial capacity of the service provider to fulfil obligations or to
provide remedies.

children.title: (v) Legal Risk - where an RCB is subjected to, inter alia, fines, penalties, or
children.text:
(v) Legal Risk - where an RCB is subjected to, inter alia, fines, penalties, or
punitive damages resulting from supervisory actions, or private settlements due
to omissions and commissions by the service provider.

children.title: (vi) Exit Strategy Risk - may arise when an RCB becomes overly reliant on one
children.text:
(vi) Exit Strategy Risk - may arise when an RCB becomes overly reliant on one
service provider, loses relevant internal skills preventing it from bringing the
activity back in-house, or enters into contracts that make speedy exits
prohibitively expensive.

children.title: (vii) Counterparty Risk - such as where the service provider engages in
children.text:
(vii) Counterparty Risk - such as where the service provider engages in
inappropriate underwriting or credit assessments.

children.title: (viii) Country Risk - where the political, social, or legal climate creates added risk in
children.text:
(viii) Country Risk - where the political, social, or legal climate creates added risk in
the outsourcing arrangement.

children.title: (ix) Contractual Risk - where the RCB may not have the ability to enforce the
children.text:
(ix) Contractual Risk - where the RCB may not have the ability to enforce the
contract with the service provider.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (x) Concentration and Systemic Risk - where there is a lack of control of an RCB
children.text:
(x) Concentration and Systemic Risk - where there is a lack of control of an RCB
over a service provider, more so when overall banking industry has considerable
exposure to one service provider.
D.2 Confidentiality and Security of Information

children.title: 19. An RCB shall seek to ensure the security, preservation, and protection of the
children.text:
19. An RCB shall seek to ensure the security, preservation, and protection of the
customer information in the custody of the service provider.

children.title: 20. Access to customer information by a service provider or its staff shall be on a 'need
children.text:
20. Access to customer information by a service provider or its staff shall be on a 'need
to know' basis, i.e., limited to those areas where the information is required in order
to perform the outsourced function.

children.title: 21. An RCB shall review and monitor the security practices and control processes of its
children.text:
21. An RCB shall review and monitor the security practices and control processes of its
service providers on a regular basis and require the service provider to disclose
security breaches.

children.title: 22. In instances, where a service provider acts as an outsourcing agent for multiple
children.text:
22. In instances, where a service provider acts as an outsourcing agent for multiple
entities, care shall be taken to build strong safeguards so that there is no comingling
or combining of information, documents, records, and assets.

children.title: 23. An RCB shall ensure that its service providers are able to isolate and clearly identify
children.text:
23. An RCB shall ensure that its service providers are able to isolate and clearly identify
the RCB's customer information, documents, records, and assets to protect the
confidentiality of the information.

children.title: 24. An RCB shall immediately notify RBI / NABARD in the event of breach of security
children.text:
24. An RCB shall immediately notify RBI / NABARD in the event of breach of security
and leakage of confidential customer related information. In these eventualities, the
RCB shall be liable to its customers for any damage.

children.title: E. Outsourcing Process
children.text:
E. Outsourcing Process
E.1 Service Provider Evaluation

children.title: 25. An RCB shall perform appropriate due diligence while considering or renewing an
children.text:
25. An RCB shall perform appropriate due diligence while considering or renewing an
outsourcing arrangement, to assess the capability of the service provider to comply
with obligations in the outsourcing agreement on an ongoing basis.

children.title: 26. The due diligence mentioned above shall involve an evaluation of all available
children.text:
26. The due diligence mentioned above shall involve an evaluation of all available
information, as applicable, about the service provider, including but not limited to:

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (i) qualitative, quantitative, financial, operational, legal, and reputational factors;
children.text: (i) qualitative, quantitative, financial, operational, legal, and reputational factors;

children.title: (ii) risks arising from undue concentration, if outsourcing to a single service
children.text:
(ii) risks arising from undue concentration, if outsourcing to a single service
provider or a limited number of service providers;

children.title: (iii) past experience, and demonstrated competence to implement and support the
children.text:
(iii) past experience, and demonstrated competence to implement and support the
proposed activity over the contracted period;

children.title: (iv) financial soundness and ability to service commitments even under adverse
children.text:
(iv) financial soundness and ability to service commitments even under adverse
conditions;

children.title: (v) business reputation and culture, compliance, complaints, and outstanding or
children.text:
(v) business reputation and culture, compliance, complaints, and outstanding or
potential litigation;

children.title: (vi) quality of due diligence exercised by service provider of its employees and sub-
children.text:
(vi) quality of due diligence exercised by service provider of its employees and sub-
contractors;

children.title: (vii) security and internal control, audit coverage, reporting and monitoring
children.text:
(vii) security and internal control, audit coverage, reporting and monitoring
procedures, and business continuity management;

children.title: (viii) external factors like political, economic, social and legal environment of the
children.text:
(viii) external factors like political, economic, social and legal environment of the
jurisdiction in which the service provider operates and other events that may
impact data security and service performance; and

children.title: (ix) ability to effectively service all the customers with confidentiality, especially
children.text:
(ix) ability to effectively service all the customers with confidentiality, especially
where a service provider has exposure to multiple entities.

children.title: 27. Where possible, an RCB shall obtain independent reviews and market feedback on
children.text:
27. Where possible, an RCB shall obtain independent reviews and market feedback on
the service provider to supplement the findings of its own due diligence.

children.title: 28. An RCB shall also evaluate whether the systems of its service provider are
children.text:
28. An RCB shall also evaluate whether the systems of its service provider are
compatible with those of the RCB and acceptability of standards of performance
including in the area of customer service.
E.2 Outsourcing Agreement

children.title: 29. An RCB shall ensure that the terms and conditions governing the outsourcing
children.text:
29. An RCB shall ensure that the terms and conditions governing the outsourcing
arrangement are carefully defined in written agreements and vetted by the RCB's
legal counsel on their legal effect and enforceability. The agreement shall
appropriately reckon the associated risks and the strategies for mitigating or

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
managing them. The RCB shall ensure that such an agreement is sufficiently flexible
to allow the RCB to retain an appropriate level of control over the outsourcing, and
the right to intervene with appropriate measures to meet legal and regulatory
obligations. The agreement shall also bring out the nature of legal relationship
between the parties, i.e., whether agent-principal or otherwise.

children.title: 30. Some of the key provisions of the agreement shall include:
children.text: 30. Some of the key provisions of the agreement shall include:

children.title: (i) details of the activities being outsourced including Service Level Agreements
children.text:
(i) details of the activities being outsourced including Service Level Agreements
(SLAs) to agree and establish accountability for performance expectations.
SLAs shall clearly formalise the performance criteria to measure the quality and
quantity of service levels;

children.title: (ii) access by the RCB to all books, records, and information relevant to the
children.text:
(ii) access by the RCB to all books, records, and information relevant to the
outsourced activity available with the service provider;

children.title: (iii) regular and continuous monitoring and assessment by the RCB of the service
(iv) prior approval / consent of the RCB for use of subcontractors by the service
children.text:
(iv) prior approval / consent of the RCB for use of subcontractors by the service
provider for all or part of an outsourced activity. Before according the consent,
an RCB shall review the subcontracting arrangement and ensure that these
arrangements are compliant with these Directions;

children.title: (v) controls for maintaining confidentiality of data of the RCB and its customers, and
(vi) contingency plan(s) to ensure business continuity;
children.text: (vi) contingency plan(s) to ensure business continuity;

children.title: (vii) right of the RCB to conduct audits on the service provider whether by its internal
children.text:
(vii) right of the RCB to conduct audits on the service provider whether by its internal
or external auditors, or by agents appointed to act on its behalf and to obtain
copies of any audit or review reports and findings made on the service provider
in conjunction with the services performed for the RCB;

children.title: (viii) right of RBI / NABARD or persons authorised by it to access the RCB's
children.text:
(viii) right of RBI / NABARD or persons authorised by it to access the RCB's
documents, records of transactions, logs, and other necessary information given

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
to, stored or processed by the service provider, within a reasonable time. This
includes information maintained in paper and electronic formats;

children.title: (ix) right of the NABARD to cause an inspection of a service provider of an RCB and
(x) a termination clause and minimum period for executing termination;
children.text: (x) a termination clause and minimum period for executing termination;

children.title: (xi) provision that confidentiality of customers' information shall be maintained even
children.text:
(xi) provision that confidentiality of customers' information shall be maintained even
after the contract expires, or gets terminated; and

children.title: (xii) provision to ensure that the service provider preserves documents and data in
children.text:
(xii) provision to ensure that the service provider preserves documents and data in
accordance with legal and regulatory obligations of the RCB and take suitable
steps to ensure that its interests are protected in this regard even post
termination of the services.
E.3 Monitoring and Control of Outsourced Activities

children.title: 31. An RCB shall have in place a management structure to monitor and control its
children.text:
31. An RCB shall have in place a management structure to monitor and control its
outsourced activities and shall ensure that outsourcing agreements with service
providers contain provisions to address the same.

children.title: 32. An RCB shall maintain a central record of all material outsourcing of financial
children.text:
32. An RCB shall maintain a central record of all material outsourcing of financial
services for review by its Board and MD / CEO along with the Senior Management.
The records shall be updated promptly, and half yearly reviews shall be placed
before the Board.

children.title: 33. Regular audits, at least annually, by either the internal auditors or external auditors
children.text:
33. Regular audits, at least annually, by either the internal auditors or external auditors
of an RCB shall assess the adequacy of the risk management practices adopted in
overseeing and managing the outsourcing arrangement, the RCB's compliance with
its risk management framework, and the requirements of these Directions.

children.title: 34. An RCB shall, at least on an annual basis, review the financial and operational
children.text:
34. An RCB shall, at least on an annual basis, review the financial and operational
condition of the service provider to assess its ability to continue to meet its
outsourcing obligations. Such due diligence reviews, which shall be based on all
available information about the service provider, shall highlight any deterioration or

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
breach in performance standards, confidentiality, and security, and in operational
resilience or business continuity preparedness.

children.title: 35. Certain services might involve reconciliation of transaction between an RCB, its
children.text:
35. Certain services might involve reconciliation of transaction between an RCB, its
service provider, its subcontractors. In such cases, the RCB shall ensure that
reconciliation of transactions between itself and the service provider (and its
subcontractors) are carried out as advised in RBI guidelines on Outsourcing of Cash
Management - Reconciliation of Transactions' dated May 14, 2019, as applicable
and as amended from time to time.
E.4 Business Continuity and Management of Disaster Recovery Plan

children.title: 36. An RCB shall require its service providers to develop and establish a robust
children.text:
36. An RCB shall require its service providers to develop and establish a robust
framework for documenting, maintaining, and testing business continuity and
recovery procedures. It shall ensure that the service provider periodically tests the
Business Continuity and Recovery Plan and may also consider joint testing and
recovery exercises with its service provider at mutually agreed frequency, but at
least annually.

children.title: 37. In establishing a viable contingency plan, an RCB shall consider the availability of
children.text:
37. In establishing a viable contingency plan, an RCB shall consider the availability of
alternative service providers or the possibility of bringing the outsourced activity back
in-house in an emergency, and the costs, time and resources that would be
involved.

children.title: 38. An RCB shall ensure that its service providers are able to isolate the RCB's
children.text:
38. An RCB shall ensure that its service providers are able to isolate the RCB's
information, documents, and records, and other assets so that in adverse conditions
or termination of the agreement, all documents, records of transactions and
information given to the service provider and assets of the RCB can be removed
from the possession of the service provider (in order to enable the RCB to continue
its business operations), deleted, destroyed, or rendered unusable.

children.title: 39. In order to mitigate the risk of unexpected termination of the outsourcing agreement
children.text:
39. In order to mitigate the risk of unexpected termination of the outsourcing agreement
or insolvency or liquidation of its service provider, an RCB shall retain an appropriate
level of control over its outsourcing arrangement along with the right to intervene
with appropriate measures to continue its business operations without incurring

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
prohibitive expenses and disruption in the operations of the RCB and its services to
the customers.
E.5 Termination

children.title: 40. If the services of a service provider are terminated by an RCB, then it shall:
children.text: 40. If the services of a service provider are terminated by an RCB, then it shall:

children.title: (i) publicise the same by displaying at a prominent place in its branches and by
children.text:
(i) publicise the same by displaying at a prominent place in its branches and by
posting it on the website so as to ensure that its customers do not continue to
deal with the service provider; and

children.title: (ii) inform Indian Banks' Association (IBA) of the reasons for termination to enable
children.text:
(ii) inform Indian Banks' Association (IBA) of the reasons for termination to enable
IBA to maintain a caution list of such service providers for sharing among banks.

children.title: F. Specific Outsourcing Arrangements
children.text:
F. Specific Outsourcing Arrangements
F.1 Offshore outsourcing

children.title: 41. In principle, outsourcing arrangements shall only be entered into with parties
children.text:
41. In principle, outsourcing arrangements shall only be entered into with parties
operating in jurisdictions that uphold confidentiality agreements and clauses

children.title: 42. While engaging with service provider(s) in a foreign country, an RCB shall:
children.text: 42. While engaging with service provider(s) in a foreign country, an RCB shall:

children.title: (i) closely monitor government policies of the jurisdiction in which the service
children.text:
(i) closely monitor government policies of the jurisdiction in which the service
provider is based and political, social, economic, and legal conditions, both
during the risk assessment and on a continuous basis, and establish sound
procedures for dealing with country risk. This includes having appropriate
contingency and exit strategies;

children.title: (ii) clearly specify the governing law of the outsourcing arrangement;
children.text: (ii) clearly specify the governing law of the outsourcing arrangement;

children.title: (iii) ensure that the availability of records to the RCB and the RBI / NABARD will not
(iv) ensure activities outsourced outside India are conducted in a manner so as not
<image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (v) ensure that, where the offshore service provider is a regulated entity, the
children.text:
(v) ensure that, where the offshore service provider is a regulated entity, the
relevant offshore regulator will neither obstruct the arrangement nor object to the
NABARD's inspection or RBI / NABARD's visits or visits of RCB's internal or
external auditors;

children.title: (vi) ensure that the regulatory authority of the offshore location does not have
(vii) ensure that the jurisdiction of the courts in the offshore location where data is
children.text:
(vii) ensure that the jurisdiction of the courts in the offshore location where data is
maintained does not extend to the operations of the RCB in India on the
strength of the fact that the data is being processed there even though the
actual transactions are undertaken in India; and

children.title: (viii) ensure that all original records continue to be maintained in India.
children.text: (viii) ensure that all original records continue to be maintained in India.

children.title: 43. The overseas outsourcing operations of an RCB shall be governed by both, these
children.text:
43. The overseas outsourcing operations of an RCB shall be governed by both, these
Directions and the host country guidelines, and in case there are differences, the
more stringent of the two shall prevail. However, where there is any conflict, the host
country guidelines shall prevail.

children.title: G. Redressal of Grievances related to Outsourced Services
children.text: G. Redressal of Grievances related to Outsourced Services

children.title: 44. Outsourcing arrangements entered into by an RCB shall not affect the rights of its
children.text:
44. Outsourcing arrangements entered into by an RCB shall not affect the rights of its
customers against the RCB, including the ability of the customers to obtain redressal
as applicable under relevant laws.

children.title: 45. In cases where customers are required to deal with a service provider in the process
children.text:
45. In cases where customers are required to deal with a service provider in the process
of dealing with an RCB, it shall incorporate a clause in the corresponding product
literature, and brochures, stating that services of the service provider, including in
sales, and marketing, of the products may be used. The role of the service provider
may be indicated in broad terms.

children.title: 46. An RCB shall have a robust grievance redressal mechanism that shall not be
children.text:
46. An RCB shall have a robust grievance redressal mechanism that shall not be
compromised in any manner on account of outsourcing, i.e., responsibility for
redressal of customers' grievances related to outsourced services shall rest with the
RCB. In case of microfinance loans, a declaration that the RCB shall be accountable

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
for inappropriate behaviour by its employees or employees of the outsourced agency
and shall provide timely grievance redressal, shall be made in the loan agreement,
and in the Fair Practices Code (FPC) displayed in its office / branch premises /
website.

children.title: 47. In addition to the above:
children.text: 47. In addition to the above:

children.title: (i) an RCB shall constitute Grievance Redressal Machinery in accordance with the
children.text:
(i) an RCB shall constitute Grievance Redressal Machinery in accordance with the
provisions of Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Responsible
Business Conduct) Directions, 2025 and give wide publicity about it within the
bank through electronic and print media and also by placing the information on its
website;

children.title: (ii) the name and contact number of designated grievance redressal officer of the
children.text:
(ii) the name and contact number of designated grievance redressal officer of the
RCB shall be made known and widely publicised. The designated officer shall
ensure that genuine grievances of customers are redressed promptly without
involving delay. It shall be clearly indicated that RCB's Grievance Redressal
Machinery will also deal with the issues relating to services provided by the
service provider;

children.title: (iii) the grievance redressal procedure of the RCB and the time frame for responding
children.text:
(iii) the grievance redressal procedure of the RCB and the time frame for responding
to the complaints (maximum 30 days) shall be placed on the RCB's website; and

children.title: (iv) if a complaint is rejected wholly or partly by an RCB, and the complainant is not
children.text:
(iv) if a complaint is rejected wholly or partly by an RCB, and the complainant is not
satisfied with the reply or does not get any reply within 30 days, after the RCB
received the complaint, the complainant shall have the option of approaching the
Consumer Education and Protection Cell (CEPC) of respective Regional Office of
RBI for redressal of their grievance(s).

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: Chapter IV - Repeal and Other Provisions
hierarchy.text: Chapter IV - Repeal and Other Provisions

children.title: A. Repeal and saving
children.text: A. Repeal and saving

children.title: 48. With the issue of these Directions, the existing Directions, instructions, and
children.text:
48. With the issue of these Directions, the existing Directions, instructions, and
guidelines relating to outsourcing of financial services, as applicable to RCBs, stand
repealed, as communicated vide circular DOR.RRC.REC.302/33-01-010/2025-26
dated November 28, 2025. The Directions, instructions, and guidelines repealed
prior to the issuance of these Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

children.title: 48. Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken, or
children.text:
48. Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken, or
initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to
be governed by the provisions thereof. All approvals or acknowledgments granted
under these repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these
Directions. Further, the repeal of these Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
not in any way prejudicially affect:
(1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder;
(2) any, penalty, forfeiture, or punishment incurred in respect of any contravention
committed thereunder;
(3) any investigation, legal proceeding, or remedy in respect of any such right,
privilege, obligation, liability, penalty, forfeiture, or punishment as aforesaid;
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.

children.title: B. Application of other laws not barred
children.text: B. Application of other laws not barred

children.title: 49. The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of the
children.text:
49. The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of the
provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in
force.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: C. Interpretations
children.text: C. Interpretations

children.title: 50. For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
children.text:
50. For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary clarifications in
respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any provision of these
Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
(Sunil T S Nair)
Chief General Manager
